article_meta.title: President Donald Trump dismissed sex offender Jeffrey Epstein as “disgusting” and decried his procurer, Ghislaine Maxwel

transcript.content:
You are a news summarization assistant. Summarize the following article objectively and extract its key facts. Respond ONLY with a valid JSON object — no prose, no markdown fences:
{
  "summary": "string (150-200 words, neutral tone)",
  "key_facts": ["string","string","string","string","string"],
  "sources_represented": ["string"]
}
Rules:
- summary must be 150-200 words
- key_facts must contain EXACTLY 5 items — the 5 most objectively significant facts
- sources_represented must list every distinct perspective or source type included
- Do not editorialize, adopt the article's framing, or use loaded language
- Represent all perspectives present in the article proportionally

Summarize this article:

---
HEADLINE: President Donald Trump dismissed sex offender Jeffrey Epstein as “disgusting” and decried his procurer, Ghislaine Maxwel
SOURCE: Breitbart Fact Check

ARTICLE:

President Donald Trump dismissed sex offender Jeffrey Epstein as “disgusting” and decried his procurer, Ghislaine Maxwell as “evil” back in 2006, a now-retired cop informed FBI agents in 2019. The revelation is contained in a newly unsealed court document released Monday by the Department of Justice. CNBC reports Trump personally called the then-Palm Beach Police Chief Michael Reiter “to tell him ‘thank goodness you’ [are] stopping [Epstein], everyone has known he’s been doing this,” Reiter told the FBI in October 2019, according to the FBI document, known as a 302. The document stated whistleblower Trump “was one of the very first people to call” the police when word spread Epstein was under investigation. This simple fact defies the president’s opponents who have since spread lies and falsehoods about his relationship with Epstein and convicted associate Maxwell. Trump said in the call he distanced himself from Epstein, according to the document, claiming he “got the hell out of there” when around Epstein with teenagers present. The outlet notes, “Reiter’s name is redacted from the 302. But the document identifies the interview subject as the person who had been Palm Beach’s police chief at the time of the department’s investigation of Epstein, who was Reiter.” Reiter told the Miami Herald, which first reported the document, Trump called him in 2006, after the police department’s probe of Epstein became publicly known. Trump told Reiter he had thrown Epstein out of his club, Mar-a-Lago, which is located in Palm Beach, the summary said. RELATED: RELEASE THE FILES! Trump Says “The Democrats Were Epstein’s Friends” “Trump told him [Reiter] people in New York knew Epstein was disgusting,” according to the FBI’s 302 summary of its interview with Reiter. “Trump said Maxwell was Epstein’s operative, ‘she is evil and to focus on her,’ ” the FBI said in the 302. The document came to light hours after Maxwell’s lawyer called on Trump to grant her executive clemency so she could speak “honestly” about what she knows. Maxwell, earlier Monday, repeatedly refused to testify to a House committee. Trump has consistently denied any wrongdoing in relation to Epstein, with whom he says he severed contact decades ago, and has not been accused of any crimes by Epstein’s victims. Follow Simon Kent on Twitter: Follow @SunSimonKent or e-mail to: skent@breitbart.com
---

{
  "summary": "A newly unsealed Department of Justice court document reveals that President Donald Trump called former Palm Beach Police Chief Michael Reiter in 2006 to express support for the police investigation into Jeffrey Epstein. According to the FBI document, Trump described Epstein as 'disgusting' and Ghislaine Maxwell as 'evil,' stating he distanced himself from Epstein after observing concerning behavior involving teenagers. The FBI’s summary of its 2019 interview with Reiter notes Trump was among the first to contact authorities when Epstein was under investigation. The document also indicates Trump claimed he had barred Epstein from his Mar-a-Lago club. These details surfaced shortly after Maxwell’s lawyer requested executive clemency from Trump so Maxwell could speak freely about the case; Maxwell has declined to testify before a House committee. Trump has consistently denied any inappropriate behavior regarding Epstein and has not been accused of crimes by Epstein’s victims. The Miami Herald and CNBC first reported on the newly released court filings.",

    "A court document unsealed by the Department of Justice details a 2006 call from Donald Trump to then-Palm Beach Police Chief Michael Reiter about Jeffrey Epstein.",
    "Trump reportedly described Epstein as 'disgusting' and Maxwell as 'evil,' urging police to focus on Maxwell.",
    "The FBI document states that Trump claimed to have distanced himself from Epstein and expelled him from Mar-a-Lago.",
    "The document was made public shortly after Ghislaine Maxwell’s lawyer requested clemency from Trump so she could provide information.",
    "Trump has consistently denied wrongdoing regarding Epstein and has not been accused of any crimes by Epstein’s victims."
  ],
  "sources_represented": [

    "Statements from Michael Reiter (Palm Beach Police Chief)",
    "Breitbart",
    "CNBC",
    "Miami Herald",
    "Legal representation for Ghislaine Maxwell"
  ]
}

parsed_output.summary: A newly unsealed Department of Justice court document reveals that President Donald Trump called former Palm Beach Police Chief Michael Reiter in 2006 to express support for the police investigation into Jeffrey Epstein. According to the FBI document, Trump described Epstein as 'disgusting' and Ghislaine Maxwell as 'evil,' stating he distanced himself from Epstein after observing concerning behavior involving teenagers. The FBI’s summary of its 2019 interview with Reiter notes Trump was among the first to contact authorities when Epstein was under investigation. The document also indicates Trump claimed he had barred Epstein from his Mar-a-Lago club. These details surfaced shortly after Maxwell’s lawyer requested executive clemency from Trump so Maxwell could speak freely about the case; Maxwell has declined to testify before a House committee. Trump has consistently denied any inappropriate behavior regarding Epstein and has not been accused of crimes by Epstein’s victims. The Miami Herald and CNBC first reported on the newly released court filings.
key_facts: A court document unsealed by the Department of Justice details a 2006 call from Donald Trump to then-Palm Beach Police Chief Michael Reiter about Jeffrey Epstein.
Trump reportedly described Epstein as 'disgusting' and Maxwell as 'evil,' urging police to focus on Maxwell.
The FBI document states that Trump claimed to have distanced himself from Epstein and expelled him from Mar-a-Lago.
The document was made public shortly after Ghislaine Maxwell’s lawyer requested clemency from Trump so she could provide information.
Trump has consistently denied wrongdoing regarding Epstein and has not been accused of any crimes by Epstein’s victims.
sources_represented: Statements from Michael Reiter (Palm Beach Police Chief)
